title: upaddocc
text: upaddocc Create an empty occurrence of an entity. Explain this code long upaddocc (unsigned char*Entity, longOccnum); Copy Parameters Entity Name of the entity. You can specify zero or an empty string to denote the current entity Occnum sequence number of the new occurrence. Specify -1 to append a new occurrence at the end of the current hitlist, or zero to insert the occurrence before the current occurrence. Return Values Table 1. Values Returned by upaddocc Value Meaning >0 The sequence number of the newly created occurrence. -1 The occurrence could not be created. Most likely causes: Occnum is out of range, or Entity does not exist, or is not painted on the component. Use Library: yrtl Description upaddocc creates an occurrence of Entity with sequence number Occnum . The argument Entity contains the name of the entity in which the occurrence will be created. Specify zero or an empty string to denote the current entity. upaddocc is equivalent to the creocc ProcScript statement, where you can find more information about the rules for creating occurrences. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void ADDOCC () { upaddocc(0, -1); /* Append occurrence to hitlist of current entity */ } Copy Related Links updelocc Remove an occurrence of the specified entity from the hitlist. updisocc Discard one or more occurrences of the specified entity from the hitlist. upsetocc Make the specified occurrence current.
